_meta.description: Peak heat loss due to air infiltration at design conditions per CSA F280-12 Section 5.2. Converts the annual air leakage calculation from VolumeMetricsNodes to a peak instantaneous value using the heating design temperature difference instead of degree-days. Uses the same NRL50/N-factor methodology already implemented in VolumeMetricsNodes.js (i_103).

output.note: Peak instantaneous infiltration heat loss at design conditions

preconditions.description: N-factor must be positive for valid calculation
Must have envelope area for infiltration

invariants.description: Peak infiltration heat loss cannot be negative

behavior.note: 1.21 = volumetric heat capacity of air (W·s/L·K). The NRL50/N-factor converts ACH50 to natural air change rate.
Cannot calculate without valid N-factor or area

rationale.conversionFromAnnual: The annual formula in VolumeMetricsNodes i_103 is: (1.21 × NRL50 × area / N × HDD × 24) / 1000 (kWh/yr). The F280 peak formula removes the degree-day integration and uses instantaneous deltaT: 1.21 × NRL50 × area / N × deltaT (Watts). The factor 1.21 represents air density × specific heat = 1.204 × 1005 / 1000 = 1.21 W·s/(L·K).

where.NRL50: Normalized leakage rate at 50Pa (L/s/m2) from g_108
where.N: N-factor from g_110 (climate zone, shielding, stories)
where.T_design: Outdoor heating design temperature (degC) from d_23

formula.legacy: New peak calculation derived from VolumeMetricsNodes.js i_103 annual formula

references.source: CSA F280-12 Section 5.2.5 - Air Leakage Heat Loss
VolumeMetricsNodes.js i_103 - annual air leakage heat loss formula
